title: Implement DuckDB relation binding and size guards
description:
Context:
verify batch execution depends on deterministic relation binding over supported file formats. This issue owns format detection, loading, and the size-guard behavior before rule semantics are applied.

Deliverables:
- CSV / JSON / JSONL / Parquet binding paths in verify-duckdb
- extension-based format detection and E_FORMAT_DETECT refusal path
- max-bytes enforcement before loading and max-rows enforcement after materialization
- stable binding metadata that later execution/report code can consume
acceptance_criteria:
1. Supported formats materialize deterministically through one binding surface.
2. Unsupported or ambiguous formats refuse cleanly.
3. max-bytes and max-rows guards are implemented without changing rule semantics.
4. This issue does not own query_zero_rows or lock verification yet.
notes:
Target files: crates/verify-duckdb/src/{lib.rs,bindings.rs} and binding-focused fixtures/tests.
Reservation guidance: keep file-loading concerns in verify-duckdb; do not pull them up into CLI or engine layers.

title: Lock verify protocol schemas and core domain types
description:
Context:
verify needs one canonical contract for compiled constraints, reports, refusal envelopes, and deterministic ordering. This issue owns the protocol center only.

Deliverables:
- verify.constraint.v1 schema and serde/domain types
- verify.report.v1 schema and serde/domain types
- refusal enums / structs that match the plan's refusal table and detail schemas
- canonical ordering helpers for rule results and affected entries
- example fixtures proving the compiled artifacts round-trip
acceptance_criteria:
1. verify.constraint.v1 and verify.report.v1 are encoded in both schemas and Rust types.
2. key_fields, policy_signals, and refusal envelopes are modeled explicitly.
3. Canonical ordering is defined below the CLI layer.
4. This issue does not implement DuckDB loading or rule execution logic.
notes:
Target files: schemas/verify.constraint.v1.schema.json, schemas/verify.report.v1.schema.json, crates/verify-core/src/{lib.rs,constraint.rs,report.rs,refusal.rs,order.rs}, tests/schema_contract.rs.
Reservation guidance: this issue owns protocol structs and schemas; later tasks should consume them rather than redefining shapes locally.

title: Build compile, validate, and schema command shell
description:
Context:
verify has a richer command surface than a single run command. Establish the compile, validate, and schema/discovery shell without yet implementing all compilation semantics.

Deliverables:
- command handlers or module surfaces for compile, compile --check, compile --schema, validate, and --schema
- clear separation between compiled-artifact validation and authoring validation
- command-level routing stubs that later compiler work can fill in
acceptance_criteria:
1. The repo has explicit module surfaces for compile, validate, and schema printing.
2. The command boundary distinguishes compiled-constraint validation from authoring validation.
3. This issue does not yet implement real JSON/YAML/SQL compilation semantics.
notes:
Target files: crates/verify-cli/src/{compile.rs,validate.rs} plus compile/validate command tests.
Reservation guidance: establish the subcommand shell here; later compiler tasks should fill behavior into the existing files instead of creating parallel command modules.

title: Wire verify run CLI shell and exit/refusal plumbing
description:
Context:
Implement the top-level verify run command shell: argument parsing, exit-code mapping, and refusal plumbing for PASS / FAIL / REFUSAL. Stop before real rule execution or rendering semantics.

Deliverables:
- clap surface for verify run and the arity-1 shortcut arguments
- PASS / FAIL / REFUSAL exit-code mapping at the binary boundary
- refusal plumbing from core types into CLI command handling
- help and version output wired through the binary shell
acceptance_criteria:
1. Help and version output match the plan closely.
2. PASS / FAIL / REFUSAL exit mapping is defined at the CLI boundary.
3. The shell is exercisable without real engine logic behind it.
4. This issue does not own report rendering or real evaluation semantics.
notes:
Target files: crates/verify-cli/src/{main.rs,run.rs} and CLI-focused tests.
Reservation guidance: keep argument parsing and exit/refusal mapping out of render.rs and engine internals.

title: Implement portable aggregate and relational rule engine
description:
Context:
Implement the remaining portable rule family that covers aggregates and relation-to-relation integrity.

Deliverables:
- row_count
- aggregate_compare
- foreign_key
- deterministic failure localization and ordering for these rule kinds
- shared helper surfaces that later embedded execution can reuse
acceptance_criteria:
1. row_count, aggregate_compare, and foreign_key match the compiled rule contract exactly.
2. Aggregate and relational checks produce localized failures, not just boolean verdicts.
3. Behavior is portable and not expressed as DuckDB-specific query text.
4. This issue does not own query_zero_rows.
notes:
Target files: crates/verify-engine/src/portable_relation.rs and relation-focused tests.
Reservation guidance: keep portable relational semantics here so embedded execution can reuse them directly.

title: Implement lock verification and input_verification results
description:
Context:
verify optionally checks bound inputs against lockfiles before evaluation. This issue owns membership verification, drift detection, and the structured input_verification surface consumed by reports.

Deliverables:
- repeatable --lock loading path
- bound-input membership checks against provided locks
- content-hash drift detection
- structured input_verification results for successful runs and refusal details for failures
acceptance_criteria:
1. Missing lock membership refuses with E_INPUT_NOT_LOCKED.
2. Hash drift refuses with E_INPUT_DRIFT.
3. Successful verification yields structured input_verification data.
4. This issue does not own final human rendering.
notes:
Target files: crates/verify-duckdb/src/lock_check.rs and lock-focused tests/fixtures.
Reservation guidance: keep lock checks as a library surface that integration and rendering can consume later.

title: Implement verify v0 constraint primitive
description:
Context:
verify now has a top-tier implementation plan in docs/plan.md, but the repo still contains no code. This epic tracks delivery of the verify v0 constraint primitive as a reusable spine/factory protocol, not as a one-off CLI script.

Scope:
- compiled constraint artifact: verify.constraint.v1
- compiled report artifact: verify.report.v1
- one primitive across arity-1 and arity-N execution
- portable rules plus explicit batch-only query rules
- batch executor, embedded executor, and deterministic reporting

Non-goals:
- benchmark scoring
- policy decisions owned by assess
- graph validation or hosted orchestration
- long-term provenance beyond lock/pack participation and local witness receipts

Exit criteria:
- the D1-D9 stages in docs/plan.md are satisfied
- verify has a swarm-safe crate layout, fixture corpus, and named test suites
- batch and embedded portable-rule parity is proven
- quality gates and determinism checks are green

title: Implement report summary math and rendering surfaces
description:
Context:
verify is a report tool. This issue owns summary math, severity-band derivation, and the JSON/human rendering surfaces over the shared report model.

Deliverables:
- summary counts and by_severity math
- policy_signals.severity_band derivation
- JSON serialization matching verify.report.v1
- compact human output for PASS / FAIL / REFUSAL
acceptance_criteria:
1. Human and JSON outputs derive from the same report model.
2. summary.by_severity counts failing rules only.
3. One result entry exists per rule and PASS entries carry violation_count = 0.
4. Rendering does not re-implement rule semantics.
notes:
Target files: crates/verify-engine/src/summary.rs, crates/verify-cli/src/render.rs, rendering-focused tests.
Reservation guidance: summary math lives below the CLI shell; render.rs should format existing report data rather than inventing semantics.

title: Bootstrap verify workspace, crates, and swarm-safe file skeleton
description:
Context:
verify currently has only docs. Create the workspace, crate, schema, fixture, and test file skeleton that matches docs/plan.md so later agents can reserve exact files instead of improvising structure.

Deliverables:
- root Cargo.toml workspace plus crate Cargo.toml files for verify-core, verify-engine, verify-duckdb, and verify-cli
- skeleton source files for verify-core, verify-engine, verify-duckdb, and verify-cli that match the plan's module split
- schema, fixture, and test paths from the plan created in the repo
- compileable shell only; no real verify semantics yet
acceptance_criteria:
1. cargo test compiles a shell workspace successfully.
2. Every planned crate and source file path exists.
3. The file map enables later agents to reserve one or two files instead of whole crates.
4. This issue does not absorb real rule semantics, DuckDB logic, or report rendering.
notes:
Target files: Cargo.toml, crates/*/Cargo.toml, crates/**/src/*.rs, schemas placeholders, fixtures placeholders, tests/*.rs placeholders.
Reservation guidance: foundation owns creation and zero-logic wiring only; later tasks should fill semantics in their assigned files rather than reshaping the layout.

title: Implement portable row and column rule engine
description:
Context:
Implement the row-oriented portable rule family that does not depend on joins or cross-relation aggregate reasoning.

Deliverables:
- unique
- not_null
- predicate
- localized failing rows with canonical ordering
- reusable evaluation helpers that stay below the CLI layer
acceptance_criteria:
1. unique, not_null, and predicate behave exactly as the plan defines.
2. Failures localize to bindings plus keys and fields when available.
3. Repeated runs over identical inputs preserve result ordering.
4. This issue does not absorb row_count, aggregate_compare, or foreign_key work.
notes:
Target files: crates/verify-engine/src/portable_row.rs and portable-row tests.
Reservation guidance: keep row/column rules isolated here; cross-relation logic belongs to portable_relation.rs.

title: Implement portable authoring compilation to verify.constraint.v1
description:
Context:
The compile shell needs real portable authoring semantics. This issue owns JSON/YAML authoring normalization into verify.constraint.v1 for portable rules only.

Deliverables:
- parse simple JSON/YAML portable rule authoring inputs
- normalize authoring into compiled verify.constraint.v1 artifacts
- enforce explicit rule IDs, severities, portability, and bindings during compilation
- support compile --check over the same path without writing output
acceptance_criteria:
1. Portable authoring inputs compile deterministically into verify.constraint.v1.
2. Missing IDs, severities, bindings, or malformed rule payloads refuse deterministically.
3. compile --check validates the same semantics without writing output.
4. This issue does not own SQL-backed query rule compilation.
notes:
Target files: crates/verify-cli/src/compile.rs plus portable-authoring fixtures/tests.
Reservation guidance: extend the existing compile surface; do not create a second compiler entrypoint.

title: Add embedded executor and batch/embedded parity proof
description:
Context:
verify is also a factory primitive. Add the embedded executor surface and prove that portable rules produce identical semantics in batch and embedded execution.

Deliverables:
- embedded relation-binding API over in-memory named relations
- refusal path for batch_only rules in embedded mode
- parity tests showing portable-rule equivalence between batch and embedded execution
acceptance_criteria:
1. Embedded execution can run portable rules without filesystem assumptions.
2. Batch-only rules refuse with E_BATCH_ONLY_RULE in embedded mode.
3. Portable-rule results are identical between batch and embedded execution for the same relation contents.
4. This issue does not implement winner selection or policy logic.
notes:
Target files: crates/verify-engine/src/embedded.rs and tests/embedding_equivalence.rs.
Reservation guidance: keep the embedded API narrow and protocol-centric; do not let factory-specific orchestration concerns leak into verify.

title: Integrate end-to-end verify batch command paths
description:
Context:
This is the composition step. Wire the existing modules into working verify batch command paths without inventing new architecture.

Deliverables:
- end-to-end verify run over compiled constraints for arity-1 and arity-N cases
- arity-1 shortcut path using compile+run semantics
- PASS / FAIL / REFUSAL end-to-end coverage over shared fixtures
- witness append integration on normal run paths
acceptance_criteria:
1. verify run succeeds end to end on one PASS fixture, one FAIL fixture, and one REFUSAL fixture.
2. The arity-1 shortcut behaves as compile+run, not as a separate execution engine.
3. Integration code reuses existing module APIs instead of duplicating behavior in main.
4. Witness append happens on run paths without changing domain outcomes.
notes:
Target files: crates/verify-cli/src/{main.rs,run.rs}, end-to-end CLI tests, and minimal glue code elsewhere.
Reservation guidance: this is composition work; prefer calling existing module APIs over reopening lower-level semantics files.

title: Close verify quality gates and determinism proof
description:
Context:
This final issue proves the implementation against the named verify quality gates. It is stop-ship verification, not architecture discovery.

Deliverables:
- determinism proof showing stable JSON bytes across repeated runs on the same fixtures
- full quality-gate pass for schema validation, portable rules, query rules, locks, CLI, and embedded parity
- documented pass of fmt, clippy, cargo test, and ubs
- any final acceptance mismatches resolved without expanding v0 scope
acceptance_criteria:
1. All quality gates in docs/plan.md are explicitly satisfied.
2. Repeated JSON runs over the same fixture bytes are byte-identical.
3. fmt, clippy, cargo test, and ubs all pass.
4. No late fixes expand v0 scope or bypass the compiled protocol contract.
notes:
Target files: tests/determinism.rs, any final gate-oriented tests, and minimal follow-up fixes needed to satisfy the gates.
Reservation guidance: prove the system; do not redesign modules here.

title: Establish verify fixture corpus and named test harness
description:
Context:
The swarm needs a stable fixture tree and named test suites before behavior work starts landing. This issue owns the shared corpus and harness shape, not final feature completeness.

Deliverables:
- fixture layout under fixtures/authoring, fixtures/constraints, fixtures/inputs, fixtures/locks, and fixtures/reports
- named test files matching the plan: schema_contract, portable_rules, query_rules, refusals, lock_integration, cli, embedding_equivalence, determinism
- representative arity-1 and arity-N happy-path fixtures plus malformed/refusal fixture families
- stable fixture naming conventions that later agents can extend without relocating files
acceptance_criteria:
1. The fixture tree and named test files exist and compile.
2. There is at least one arity-1 fixture family, one arity-N fixture family, and one refusal fixture family.
3. Later agents can add cases without changing the harness layout.
4. This issue does not claim final rule semantics by itself.
notes:
Target files: fixtures/**, tests/{schema_contract,portable_rules,query_rules,refusals,lock_integration,cli,embedding_equivalence,determinism}.rs.
Reservation guidance: harness shape and fixture naming live here; feature lanes should add cases under the established layout instead of inventing new trees.

title: Implement local witness receipt plumbing and query surface
description:
Context:
verify participates in the standard local witness receipt pattern. Implement witness append/query behavior as a local receipt log only, consistent with the narrowed spine boundary.

Deliverables:
- append-on-run witness write path with non-fatal failures
- witness query / last / count surfaces
- outcome mapping for PASS / FAIL / REFUSAL runs
- no attempt to turn witness into portable evidence or lock/pack authority
acceptance_criteria:
1. Run commands can append witness receipts without changing domain outcomes on witness failure.
2. witness query / last / count are implemented as read-only surfaces.
3. Witness remains local-only supplemental context.
4. This issue does not change lock or pack semantics.
notes:
Target files: crates/verify-cli/src/witness.rs and witness-focused tests/fixtures.
Reservation guidance: keep witness local and non-authoritative; do not spread witness concerns into constraint or report schemas.

title: Implement query_zero_rows compilation and batch execution
description:
Context:
Batch-only query rules are the explicit escape hatch for SQL-heavy checks. This issue owns both the SQL-backed compile path and the runtime execution path for query_zero_rows.

Deliverables:
- compile named SQL assertions into batch_only query_zero_rows rules
- execute compiled query_zero_rows rules over bound DuckDB relations
- map SQL failures and SQL execution refusals into the documented report/refusal surfaces
- keep zero-rows-means-PASS semantics explicit
acceptance_criteria:
1. Named SQL assertions compile into one batch_only rule per assertion.
2. query_zero_rows returns PASS on zero rows and FAIL with localized rows otherwise.
3. SQL execution failures map deterministically to E_SQL_ERROR.
4. This issue does not silently promote query rules into embedded-compatible semantics.
notes:
Target files: crates/verify-cli/src/compile.rs, crates/verify-duckdb/src/query_rules.rs, query-rule fixtures/tests.
Reservation guidance: extend the existing compile path and DuckDB query executor; do not create a second rule family outside verify.constraint.v1.
